overview: Simplifies the addition or removal of inter-segment whitespace when translating to or from Chinese or Japanese scripts that do not typically use spaces. When translating from a space-delimited language (e.g., English) to a non-space-delimited language, whitespace following segment-ending punctuation is removed. When translating in the opposite direction, whitespace is added. The step performs no action when both source and target are space-delimited, or when translating between Chinese and Japanese.

punctuation.help:
Comma-separated list of punctuation classes to apply whitespace correction to. Each class handles **both** the full-width/ideographic form and the ASCII form, converting between them based on translation direction:

- `FULL_STOP` — Ideographic Full Stop (U+3002) and Full-width Full Stop (U+FF0E) ↔ period (U+002E)
- `COMMA` — Ideographic Comma (U+3001) and Full-width Comma (U+FF0C) ↔ comma (U+002C)
- `EXCLAMATION_MARK` — Full-width Exclamation Mark (U+FF01) ↔ exclamation point (U+0021)
- `QUESTION_MARK` — Full-width Question Mark (U+FF1F) ↔ question mark (U+003F)

In addition to adjusting whitespace, the step **converts the punctuation characters themselves** between full-width/ideographic and ASCII forms.
punctuation.values: Comma-separated list of punctuation class names: `FULL_STOP`, `COMMA`, `EXCLAMATION_MARK`, `QUESTION_MARK`.
examples: `FULL_STOP,COMMA` — only correct whitespace around periods and commas

whitespace.help:
Comma-separated list of Unicode whitespace character classes that the step recognizes and will add or remove following segment-ending punctuation. The default includes a comprehensive set of Unicode space characters.

Typically this does not need to be changed unless you want to restrict which whitespace characters are affected.
whitespace.values: Comma-separated list of Unicode whitespace character names. Available values include: `SPACE`, `NO_BREAK_SPACE`, `LINE_FEED`, `CARRIAGE_RETURN`, `CHARACTER_TABULATION`, `LINE_TABULATION`, `FORM_FEED`, `NEXT_LINE`, `LINE_SEPARATOR`, `PARAGRAPH_SEPARATOR`, `EN_QUAD`, `EM_QUAD`, `EN_SPACE`, `EM_SPACE`, `THREE_PER_EM_SPACE`, `FOUR_PER_EM_SPACE`, `SIX_PER_EM_SPACE`, `FIGURE_SPACE`, `PUNCUATION_SPACE`, `THIS_SPACE`, `HAIR_SPACE`, `NARROW_NO_BREAK_SPACE`, `MEDIUM_MATHEMATICAL_SPACE`, `IDEOGRAPHIC_SPACE`, `ZERO_WIDTH_SPACE`, `ZERO_WIDTH_NON_BREAKING_SPACE`.

limitations: Relies on the assumption that each source segment contains a **single sentence** and has been translated to a single sentence in the target language.
No action is taken when both source and target are space-delimited languages (e.g., English → French).
No action is taken when translating between two non-space-delimited languages (e.g., Chinese ↔ Japanese).
processingNotes: Requires both **source language** and **target language** to be set in the pipeline to determine translation direction.
The step both adjusts whitespace **and** converts punctuation characters between full-width/ideographic and ASCII forms.
Direction of correction is determined automatically: space-delimited → non-space-delimited removes whitespace; non-space-delimited → space-delimited adds whitespace.

examples.title: English to Japanese (remove whitespace)
examples.description: When translating from English (space-delimited) to Japanese (non-space-delimited), trailing whitespace after segment-ending punctuation is removed and ASCII punctuation is converted to full-width equivalents.

examples.title: Japanese to English (add whitespace)
examples.description: When translating from Japanese (non-space-delimited) to English (space-delimited), whitespace is added after segment-ending punctuation and full-width punctuation is converted to ASCII equivalents.
